map.split_rule: Block 1 (DSA Theory + Warm-up, 1.5h) + Block 2 (DSA Implementation + Problems, 3.5h). Block 1 covers setup, resource consumption, warm-up, and concept introduction. Block 2 contains the tiered DSA problems (6-10) with interleaving. Exact split time determined at generation time based on day content — heavier theory days (e.g., Day 29 three-sorts intro) get more Block 1.
Direct 1:1. Stats concepts + Python implementation from-scratch + library verification. Tier structure NOT applied to Block 3 — Stats exercises follow curriculum content order.
Direct 1:1. Platform problems from LeetCode / StrataScratch / DataLemur. Untiered. Block 4 type menu (A-E) NOT consulted on standard days; Block 4 is uniformly 'Platform Practice'.
Direct 1:1. Review, notes, git, spaced retrieval, tomorrow preview. Block 5 spaced retrieval carries forward from Phase 1 SD-001.

break.placement: at 2.5h DSA mark (~08:30 if day starts 06:00)
break.rationale: Bönstrup et al. 2019 — skill gains occur during rest in deliberate practice

block_mapping.review_day_exception:
Review days (28, 35, 42) use a DIFFERENT block mapping, applying SD-001 intensity-reduction to Phase 2 curriculum's 4-block 11h spec. See deviation_log SD-004 for full rationale.
Curriculum review day: Morning Review (3h) + Midday Timed (3h) + Afternoon Integration (4h) + Evening Reflection (1h) = 11h.
Structure review day: R1 (hybrid quiz + gaps, ~2.5h) + R2 (spaced retrieval practice, untimed, 1.5-2h) + R3 (integrative project, ~3h) = ~6.5-7.5h structured. Evening Reflection = unstructured remainder.
Mapping: Morning Review → R1 (quiz scaled 35q→24q per SD-004) Midday Timed + partial Afternoon → R2 (10 timed → 6-10 untimed) Afternoon Integration (first 3h) → R3 Evening Reflection + remainder → UNSTRUCTURED

section.name: header
section.title: # 📅 Day [N] — [Day Title]
section.subtitle: ### Python Foundations Bootcamp | Phase 2, Week [W] | [HH:MM]–[HH:MM] IST

section.name: objectives
section.title: ## 🎯 Learning Objectives

each_objective.req: Concrete action verb (write, explain, predict, demonstrate, build, solve, analyze)
Names exact concept, function, algorithm, or statistical test
Achievable with ONLY prior knowledge + today's concepts
Single dense sentence with deliverables embedded

section.objective_coverage_rule: Each of the 10 objectives must be referenced ≥2 times outside the objectives section (Gate 5 requirement). Phase 2 hybrid track distribution guidance: - Block 2 (DSA Impl): covers 5-7 DSA objectives, 1-2x each - Block 3 (Stats Midday): covers 3-5 Stats objectives, 1-2x each - Block 4 (Platform Practice): covers 3-5 DSA objectives, 1x each - Block 5 (Spaced Retrieval): touches 2-3 objectives from prior days The COMBINATION must ensure every Phase 2 objective hits ≥2 references. Stats-objective concentration in Block 3 is structural (Gate 5 WARN expected; see SD-005).

section.name: resource_library
section.title: ## 📚 Resource Library
section.columns: Resource Name | Type | Time Budget | Link | Objective | How to Use
section.link_domains: official/stable only (docs.python.org, peps.python.org, numpy.org, pandas.pydata.org, scipy.github.io, www.statsmodels.org)
section.video_pattern: [Search: "exact title YouTube"] when URL may change
section.req: Every resource needs a "How to Use" instruction — never just "read this"
Link Verification Note at end of section

For platform problem references (LeetCode, StrataScratch, DataLemur), use search-pattern format: [Search: "problem topic site:platform.com"] unless the exact URL has been verified. Never fabricate platform problem titles or URLs. Prefer [verify on platform] tag for LeetCode problem IDs.

section.name: study_notes
section.title: ## 📖 Study Notes: [Descriptive Title] (Day [N] Concepts)
section.tone: conversational, clear — not textbook, not blog
section.order: concepts build on each other — sequence matters. DSA track first, then Stats track. Within each track, follow curriculum concept order.

per_concept.req: "Edge case that will bite you" callout

section.hybrid_track_handling: DSA concepts and Stats concepts appear in the same Study Notes section, visually separated by a subsection heading. DSA subsection first (feeds Block 2). Stats subsection second (feeds Block 3). Verification references map each subsection to its downstream block.

messy_data.format: broken code → why it breaks → the fix
messy_data.scope: MAY use out-of-scope syntax — label clearly. These illustrate real-world bugs, not practice. Phase 2: may include scipy.stats / statsmodels / heapq usages from later Phase 2 days marked previewed_readonly.

time_rules.rule: Block start/end times contiguous — no gaps, no overlaps
Every sub-block, tier, drill has explicit time windows
Item times within tier MUST sum to ≤ tier allocation; if overflow: reduce items OR extend tier + cascade downstream
Breaks sacred — never absorbed into content
Mid-DSA rest (Decision 6B) scheduled explicitly at ~2.5h DSA mark
Use block_mapping to derive template blocks from curriculum. Do not invent block content that isn't in the curriculum spec.

section.name: exercises_list
section.title: ## 📝 Exercises & Problems: Day [N] Complete List
section.content: summary tables referencing full specs in schedule
section.table: tier groupings (DSA) with time allocations — columns: Tier, #, Name, Type, Difficulty, Objective, Time
Stats exercises summary — columns: #, Name, Type, Objective, What to Verify, Done When
Platform problems summary — columns: #, Platform, Name, LC#/id, Difficulty, Objective, Time

section.name: completion_checklist
section.title: ## ✅ Day [N] Completion Checklist

items.item: Block 2 DSA problem counts met (6-10 tiered)
Block 4 Platform problems attempted (2-5) with verification log
Notes file has reference card + mistakes log
2–3 "can explain without looking" knowledge checks (1 DSA + 1-2 Stats)

section.critical: Every number MUST match actual content above

section.name: quick_reference
section.title: ## 🔗 Quick Reference Card
section.req: One-line summaries of today's core concepts (DSA + Stats)
Tables for syntax patterns, algorithm templates, statistical formulas
Self-contained — no dependency on study notes
Hybrid day card: clearly partition DSA section from Stats section

section.name: tomorrow_preview
section.title: ## 🔮 Tomorrow Preview
section.req: Names tomorrow's DSA topic + Stats topic, connects to today's work

block.name: dsa_theory_warmup
block.heading: #### Block 1: DSA Theory + Warm-up ([X]h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]
block.content: setup, warm-up, resource consumption, concept intro for the day's DSA topic
block.rule: Resources consumed just-in-time: read → immediately practice
Concept intro ends here; implementation + problems go in Block 2
block.curriculum_source: First portion of curriculum "DSA Morning" block (see block_mapping for split rule — typical 1.5h of 5h)

block.name: dsa_implementation_problems
block.heading: #### Block 2: DSA Implementation + Problems ([X]h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]
block.micro_rest_note: "Take a 10–30 second breather between problems — eyes off screen, let your brain process what you just solved."

problem_count.note: Phase 2 DSA block tiered problem count. Combined with Block 4 untiered (2-5), standard-day total is 8-15. See deviation_log SD-003 for rationale and retention- monitoring action.

block.interleaving_rule: Problems MUST interleave concept sub-topics within each tier. Do NOT group all problems of one sub-topic together. Mix so the learner must discriminate which approach to use. Carries forward from SD-001 (Rohrer & Taylor 2007: interleaving tripled delayed scores). Exception: Tier 1 warm-ups may be grouped if they serve as syntax recall for distinct sub-topics.

per_problem.el: Self-explanation prompt (Tier 2/3 only): "Before moving on: why does this approach work?"

rules.rule: Problem times MUST sum ≤ tier allocation
Mark priority problems for "running behind" scenarios
SELF-EXPLANATION on Tier 2/3 per per_problem element 7

block.curriculum_source: Majority of curriculum "DSA Morning" block (3.5h of 5h). Tiered structure derived from curriculum's problem list — interleave across sub-topics.

block.name: stats_midday
block.heading: #### Block 3: Stats Midday ([X]h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]
block.content: Stats concepts + Python implementation (from-scratch + library verification)

structure.step: Concept intro (15-30 min): theory + intuition + real-world framing
From-scratch implementation (30-45 min): manual computation before library use
Library verification (30-45 min): scipy.stats / numpy / matplotlib
Guided exercises (60-90 min): 3-5 exercises applying today's Stats concept
Interview-framing prompt: "how would you explain this in plain English?"

exercise_format.element: Code block with task + expected output

block.rule: No tier structure — follow curriculum Stats content order
Visualization mandatory when the concept has visual intuition (distributions, CLT demonstration, box plots, hypothesis test decision regions)

problem_count.note: Untiered. Platform problems drawn from curriculum spec for the day. Combined with Block 2 tiered (6-10), standard-day total is 8-15. See deviation_log SD-003.

per_problem.el: Platform + problem name + difficulty (e.g., LC #1 Easy, StrataScratch Python Easy)
Link reference (search pattern OR [verify on platform])
Objective tag (typically a DSA objective from Block 2)
Solution approach hint (1 line, no spoilers)

rules.rule: NO type menu selection on standard days — uniform role
Self-explanation prompt mandatory AFTER solving (not before): "In one sentence, what pattern did I use?"
Mark priority problems for "running behind" scenarios

block.name: evening_review_spaced_retrieval
block.heading: #### Block 5: Evening Review + Spaced Retrieval (1h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]

tasks.task: SPACED RETRIEVAL (20-30 min): Re-solve 2-3 problems from Day N-2 or N-3 without looking at previous solutions. Compare with original. Track which problems needed hints vs. solved clean. Use intervals: Day+1, Day+3, Day+7, Day+14. Phase 2 adds explicit balance: ≥1 DSA problem + ≥1 Stats problem per retrieval.
Copy Quick Reference Card into day[N]_notes.md
"Mistakes I Made Today" log (min 5 entries, DSA + Stats mix)
Self-quiz from memory (5 items, closed-book, cross-track)
Optional Anki flashcard creation (list minimum card set)

final.step: Tomorrow Preview (15 min max — read, don't study)

block.curriculum_source: Curriculum "Evening Review" block (1h direct 1:1). Phase 2 Block 5 is ONE HOUR (was 1.5-2h in Phase 1); Phase 2 redistributes the extra 30-45 min into Block 2's expanded DSA Implementation time.

type.name: Scaffolded Mini-Project

reqs.req: Per stage: spec → starter guidance → gate test (asserts) → git commit
Scope note box if approved out-of-scope concept used

type.scope_note_template: > ⚠️ SCOPE NOTE: `[concept]` is formally taught on Day [X]. Here we use ONLY `[specific usage]` as a black-box tool — you don't need to understand [underlying system] yet.
type.deliverable: completed multi-stage project, gate tests passing

type.name: Deep Practice Set

reqs.req: Independent exercises (no project structure), deeper than Block 2

type.deliverable: completed exercise set, all verifications passing

reqs.req: Answer specific questions using today's tools
Revisit existing projects using today's new concepts
Before/after comparison for at least one project

override.subtitle: ### Python Foundations Bootcamp | Phase 2, Week [W] Review | [HH:MM]–[HH:MM] IST

override.focus: integration and verification across DSA + Stats tracks — NOT new learning

override.scope: minimal — gap-fixing links only, not full block-by-block set

override.title: ## 📖 Review Quiz: Week [W] Assessment (Hybrid DSA + Stats)
override.coverage: all concepts from the week (both tracks), with 3-5 questions recycled from the prior week's quiz for spacing
override.deviation_ref: SD-004 (scaled from curriculum 35q per SD-001 research)

override.tables: quiz questions (hybrid), spaced retrieval problems (mixed), integration project stages

items.item: Quiz taken and scored (24 questions, hybrid 14 DSA + 10 Stats)
Weak areas identified and gap-fixed (both tracks)
Spaced retrieval practice completed (6-10 problems, untimed, mixed DSA/Stats)
Integrative project pushed to GitHub with README

override.scope: cumulative card for ENTIRE WEEK, not single day. Partition DSA section from Stats section.

override.title: ## 🔮 Next Week Preview
override.req: Names next week's DSA theme + Stats theme + connection to this week
override.phase_transition_note: Day 42 is the Phase 2 → Phase 3 transition checkpoint. Next week preview spans ALL of Phase 3 Week 7 (linked lists + non-parametric tests) plus the Phase 2 retrospective.

block_overrides.note: Review days use 3 structured blocks (~6.5-7.5h working). Remaining time is UNSTRUCTURED — no expectations, no guilt. Reduced from curriculum's 4-block 11h spec per SD-004.

block.name: quiz_and_gaps
block.heading: #### Block 1: Hybrid Quiz + Gap-Fixing (~2.5h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]
block.content: 24-question hybrid assessment (14 DSA + 10 Stats), score results, immediately revisit weak topics. Merged quiz + gap-fixing into single block.
block.note: 3-5 quiz questions should be recycled from the prior week (both tracks) for spacing. Remaining 19-21 cover current week.

block.name: spaced_retrieval_practice
block.heading: #### Block 2: Spaced Retrieval Practice (~1.5-2h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]

rules.rule: NOT timed — emphasis on quality and self-explanation
Problems drawn from the FULL WEEK, interleaved across BOTH tracks (DSA + Stats)
Include 2-3 problems from the PRIOR week for spacing
Minimum 3 DSA problems + 2 Stats problems in the set

block.note: Curriculum specified 10 TIMED problems; structure overrides to 6-10 UNTIMED per SD-004. Removes time pressure, adds cross-week + cross-track spacing.

block.name: integrative_project
block.heading: #### Block 3: Integrative Project (~3h) — [HH:MM]–[HH:MM]

rules.rule: Combines week's DSA + Stats concepts into single deliverable
Staged with gate tests between stages
Use Block 4 type menu (default: Type A Scaffolded Mini-Project)

includes_retrospective.item: Week reflection: strongest + weakest areas (DSA + Stats)
includes_retrospective.item_if_day_42: Phase 2 retrospective + Phase 3 transition preview (T2 boundary)

block.note: Curriculum specified 4h; structure reduces to 3h per SD-004 (same reduction as Phase 1 SD-001). Remaining day time unstructured.

formatting.code_blocks: triple backticks with language tag (python, bash, markdown) — never bare
formatting.tables: Markdown pipe syntax with header separator row
formatting.text: bold for key terms on first intro; inline backticks for functions, operators, variables, statistical symbols (σ, μ, λ, p, α, β)
formatting.structure: --- between major sections only; no HTML; no collapsibles

length.note: Phase 2 hybrid days are longer than Phase 1 (DSA + Stats + platform sections); range raised from Phase 1's 800-1500
